attempts.response:
Below is a self-contained SAPIC+ model of the described signed-and-encrypted token protocol with certification-path tag. It models:
- Roles: A (sender/signing originator) and B (recipient/verifier/decryptor).
- Messages: A sends a token including timestamp tA, nonce rA, B’s identifier, non-confidential sgnData, and B’s-public-key encryption of encData; all signed by A. A also attaches a certification path term certpath(B,A) to help B retrieve/validate A’s public key (abstracted here as a structured value checked by B).
- Primitives: public-key encryption for Bp[encData]; signatures by A on a hash of the covered data; hashing for “hash-then-sign.”
- Goals:
  - Executability.
  - Authentication: If B accepts a token as from A, then either A previously sent the matching token or A’s long-term key was revealed.
  - Secrecy: If B accepts a token carrying encData, then the adversary does not learn encData unless B’s long-term key was revealed.

```spthy
theory Signed_Encrypted_Token_With_CertPath
begin

builtins: asymmetric-encryption, signing, hashing

functions:
  certpath/2   // certpath(issuer, subject) - abstract certification path tag

/* Role A (Sender/Originator):
   - Generates fresh nonce rA and timestamp tA.
   - Prepares sgnData (non-confidential) and encData (confidential).
   - Encrypts encData under B's public key pKb.
   - Forms y = <tA, rA, pKb, sgnData, c>, signs h(y) with A's secret key.
   - Sends <certpath(pKb, pKa), y, sig>.
   Events:
     - Sent(pKa, pKb, tA, rA, sgnData, ciph) correlates with B's Accept.
     - SecretPayload(encData) marks the confidential part for secrecy properties.
*/
let A(ltKa, pKa, pKb) =
  new rA;
  new tA;
  new sgnData;
  new encData;
  let c = aenc(encData, pKb) in
  let y = <tA, rA, pKb, sgnData, c> in
  let digest = h(y) in
  let sig = sign(digest, ltKa) in
  event Sent(pKa, pKb, tA, rA, sgnData, c);
  event SecretPayload(encData);
  out(<certpath(pKb, pKa), y, sig>)

/* Role B (Recipient/Verifier):
   - Receives <path, y, sig> and parses y = <tA, rA, bname, sgnData, c>.
   - Checks intended recipient (bname = pk(ltKb)).
   - Extracts pKa from path (must be certpath(pk(ltKb), pKa)).
   - Verifies signature: verify(sig, h(y), pKa) = true.
   - Decrypts c with ltKb to recover encData.
   - Accepts on success.
   Events:
     - Accept(pKa, pk(ltKb), tA, rA, sgnData, encData) marks successful verification+decryption.
     - B may record timestamp/nonce in events for auditing.
*/
let B(ltKb) =
  in(msg);
  let <path, y, sig> = msg in
  let <tA, rA, bname, sgnData, c> = y in
  if bname = pk(ltKb) then
    let certpath(=pk(ltKb), pKa) = path in
    if verify(sig, h(y), pKa) = true then
      let encData = adec(c, ltKb) in
      event Accept(pKa, pk(ltKb), tA, rA, sgnData, encData)

/* Long-term key compromise model. */
let LtkRevealProc(s) =
  event LtkReveal(pk(s)); out(s)

/* Process:
   - Generates long-term keys for A and B, publishes public keys.
   - Allows optional long-term key leakage to model compromise.
   - Runs A (needs both pKa and pKb) and B (needs only ltKb; learns pKa from path).
*/
process:
!(
  new skA; out(pk(skA));
  !(
    new skB; out(pk(skB));
    (
      LtkRevealProc(skA) | LtkRevealProc(skB)
      |
      ( !A(skA, pk(skA), pk(skB)) | !B(skB) )
    )
  )
)

/* Lemmas */

/* Executability: a run exists where A sends and B accepts a matching token. */
lemma executability:
  exists-trace
  "Ex a b t r sd d #i #j.
      Sent(a, b, t, r, sd, aenc(d, b)) @ #i
    & Accept(a, b, t, r, sd, d)        @ #j
    & #i < #j"

/* Authenticity at B:
   If B accepts a token as coming from A with parameters (t, r, sd, d),
   then either A previously sent a matching token containing aenc(d, b),
   or A's long-term key was revealed. */
lemma authenticity_B:
  "All a b t r sd d #i.
      Accept(a, b, t, r, sd, d) @ #i
    ==>
      (Ex #j. Sent(a, b, t, r, sd, aenc(d, b)) @ #j & #j < #i)
      | (Ex #rA. LtkReveal(a) @ rA)"

/* Secrecy of encData:
   If B accepted and recovered encData = d, then the adversary cannot know d
   unless B's long-term key was revealed. */
lemma secrecy_encData:
  "All a b t r sd d #i.
      Accept(a, b, t, r, sd, d) @ #i
    ==>
      not (Ex #j. K(d) @ #j)
      | (Ex #rB. LtkReveal(b) @ rB)"

end
```

   Events:

     - B may record timestamp/nonce in events for auditing.
*/

    & #i < #j"

```spthy
theory Signed_Encrypted_Token_With_CertPath
begin

builtins: asymmetric-encryption, signing, hashing

functions:
  certpath/2,
  issuer/1,
  subject/1

equations:
  issuer(certpath(i,s)) = i,
  subject(certpath(i,s)) = s

/* Role A (Sender/Originator):
   - Generates fresh nonce rA and timestamp tA.
   - Prepares sgnData (non-confidential) and encData (confidential).
   - Encrypts encData under B's public key pKb.
   - Forms y = <tA, rA, pKb, sgnData, c>, signs h(y) with A's secret key.
   - Sends <certpath(pKb, pKa), y, sig>.
*/
let A(ltKa, pKa, pKb) =
  new rA;
  new tA;
  new sgnData;
  new encData;
  let c = aenc(encData, pKb) in
  let y = <tA, rA, pKb, sgnData, c> in
  let digest = h(y) in
  let sig = sign(digest, ltKa) in
  event Sent(pKa, pKb, tA, rA, sgnData, c);
  event SecretPayload(encData);
  out(<certpath(pKb, pKa), y, sig>);
  0

/* Role B (Recipient/Verifier):
   - Receives <path, y, sig> and parses y = <tA, rA, bname, sgnData, c>.
   - Checks intended recipient (bname = pk(ltKb)).
   - Extracts pKa from path (subject) and checks issuer(path) = pk(ltKb).
   - Verifies signature using pKa, then decrypts c with ltKb to recover encData.
   - Accepts on success.
*/
let B(ltKb) =
  in(msg);
  let <path, y, sig> = msg in
  let <tA, rA, bname, sgnData, c> = y in
  if bname = pk(ltKb) then
    let pKa = subject(path) in
    if issuer(path) = pk(ltKb) then
      if verify(sig, h(y), pKa) = true then
        let encData = adec(c, ltKb) in
        event Accept(pKa, pk(ltKb), tA, rA, sgnData, encData);
        0

/* Long-term key compromise model. */
let LtkRevealProc(s) =
  event LtkReveal(pk(s)); out(s)

/* Process:
   - Generates long-term keys for A and B, publishes public keys.
   - Allows optional long-term key leakage to model compromise.
   - Runs A (needs both pKa and pKb) and B (needs only ltKb; learns pKa from path).
*/
process:
!(
  new skA; out(pk(skA));
  !(
    new skB; out(pk(skB));
    (
      LtkRevealProc(skA) | LtkRevealProc(skB)
      |
      ( !A(skA, pk(skA), pk(skB)) | !B(skB) )
    )
  )
)

/* Lemmas */

/* Executability: a run exists where A sends and B accepts a matching token. */
lemma executability:
  exists-trace
  "Ex a b t r sd d #i #j.
      Sent(a, b, t, r, sd, aenc(d, b)) @ #i
    & Accept(a, b, t, r, sd, d)        @ #j
    & #i < #j"

/* Authenticity at B:
   If B accepts a token as coming from A with parameters (t, r, sd, d),
   then either A previously sent a matching token containing aenc(d, b),
   or A's long-term key was revealed. */
lemma authenticity_B:
  "All a b t r sd d #i.
      Accept(a, b, t, r, sd, d) @ #i
    ==>
      (Ex #j. Sent(a, b, t, r, sd, aenc(d, b)) @ #j & #j < #i)
      | (Ex #rA. LtkReveal(a) @ rA)"

/* Secrecy of encData (confidential parameters):
   If B accepted and recovered encData = d, then the adversary cannot know d
   unless B's long-term key was revealed. */
lemma secrecy_encData:
  "All a b t r sd d #i.
      Accept(a, b, t, r, sd, d) @ #i
    ==>
      not (Ex #j. K(d) @ #j)
      | (Ex #rB. LtkReveal(b) @ rB)"

end
```
